Job Title: Identity Access Management IAM Technical Business Analyst - IAM Business Analyst
Ad Text:
Eveleigh & Parramatta Locations
2 days per week in office rest WFH
6 Months Contract with strong possibility of renewal
Immediate start
$900 to $952 per day
We are seeking an experienced Identity Access Management (IAM) Technical Business Analyst to join a high-performing team delivering critical identity and access initiatives across enterprise environments.

This is a strong opportunity for a BA who can operate across both business and technical domains — someone who is confident running workshops, gathering and documenting requirements, mapping workflows and data flows, and translating business needs into clear technical and functional specifications. At the same time, you will bring solid experience in IAM, access management, integrations, and security, enabling you to work closely with engineering and cybersecurity teams on complex identity solutions.
  

Essential Experience

Proven experience as a Technical Business Analyst in complex enterprise environments
Strong experience in requirements gathering, workshop facilitation, process mapping, data mapping, and technical/functional documentation
Solid understanding of Identity and Access Management (IAM) concepts, tools, and delivery practices
Hands-on exposure to platforms such as SailPoint, Okta, Microsoft IAM / Entra ID / Active Directory, or similar
Strong knowledge of integration patterns, including IAM integration with bespoke and enterprise applications
Understanding of security and cybersecurity principles as they relate to identity, authentication, authorisation, and access management
Experience documenting APIs, data flows, system interactions, and integration specifications
Strong communication skills with the ability to engage effectively with both business stakeholders and highly technical teams
Experience working in Agile delivery environments

Highly regarded experience

Exposure to identity engineering or identity lifecycle management
Experience with access provisioning, directory services, and authentication / authorisation frameworks
Knowledge of REST APIs, JSON, enterprise integration, and application connectivity
Experience using LeanIX, Lucidchart, SAP Signavio or similar architecture / process modelling tools  

Key responsibilities

Lead business requirements gathering through workshops, stakeholder interviews, and discovery sessions
Translate requirements into high-quality functional and technical documentation
Produce process maps, workflow diagrams, system interaction models, and data flow documentation
Document current state and future state processes using tools such as LeanIX, Lucidchart, Signavio or similar
Work closely with technical teams to define IAM integration requirements across enterprise applications and platforms
Analyse and document identity lifecycle processes, including onboarding, provisioning, access changes, and deprovisioning
Support the design and documentation of IAM connectors, integration services, APIs, and access workflows
Collaborate with security, engineering, platform and application teams to ensure solutions align with cybersecurity and access control requirements
Assist with testing, issue analysis, implementation support, and delivery readiness across project phases

Why this role?

Work on meaningful IAM and cybersecurity initiatives
Join a collaborative environment across business, engineering and security teams
Be involved in both strategic analysis and technical solution delivery
Contribute to enterprise-scale identity, integration and access transformation programs
If you are a Technical BA with strong IAM experience, enjoy working across stakeholders and technical teams, and can confidently bridge the gap between business requirements and identity engineering solutions, we would love to hear from you.
  
Best method to apply is using the application button on this advert. We can be contacted on (02) •••• •025 for a confidential discussion but please ensure the resume has been sent.

Hybrid setup in Macquarie Park with flexible onsite days
Join a large-scale enterprise HR and recruitment systems transformation

The program is focused on ensuring accurate, reconciled, and business-approved data is available across testing, migration rehearsals, cutover, and go-live activities-supporting long-term workforce and reporting capability.

Role:
As a Data Migration Analyst, you will:

Prepare and maintain source-to-target data mappings with business stakeholders
Profile, cleanse, and prepare HR and recruitment data for migration
Support and execute migration load into Workday
Validate and reconcile migrated data to ensure completeness and accuracy
Support migration cycles, rehearsals, cutover, and go-live activities
Track, document, and report migration issues, risks, and progress
Work closely with SMEs, delivery teams, developers, testers, and system integrators

You will have:

Experience delivering data migration activities within enterprise HR, HCM, or ERP programs
Strong data analysis skills with excellent attention to detail
Experience creating and managing source-to-target data mappings
Hands-on experience with data profiling, cleansing, reconciliation, and validation
Experience supporting or executing migration loads within structured project environments
Proficiency using MS SQL Server, SSIS, or similar migration tools
Strong stakeholder engagement and communication skills

Nice to have:

Experience with Workday Core HCM and/or Recruitment & Onboarding
Exposure to healthcare or large regulated enterprise environments
Experience working alongside systems integrators
Understanding of data governance and data quality frameworks
Experience supporting migration rehearsals, cutover, or go-live activities

Job Title: AI Lead – Trading Systems & Agent Development
Ad Text:
Most AI systems never face reality.

Ours do.

We deploy into live trading environments where information is incomplete, conditions change quickly, and performance is measurable in real time.

We are looking for someone to build systems that improve how decisions are made and executed under pressure — systems trusted because they work, not because they sound impressive.

This role carries real ownership, direct commercial impact, and unusually fast feedback loops.

The Opportunity

You will work directly with traders and decision-makers to build AI systems that:
- identify actionable signals faster
- improve decision quality under uncertainty
- compress the time between insight and execution
- increase consistency and execution precision
- continuously improve from live outcomes

You will own systems end-to-end:
problem framing → architecture → deployment → iteration

The expectation is production impact.

What Makes This Role Different

Most AI roles sit far from commercial reality.

This one does not.

Your work will be deployed quickly, tested under live conditions, and measured against outcomes that matter.

You will operate with:
- direct access to decision-makers
- minimal bureaucracy
- high autonomy
- high accountability
- tight feedback loops
- meaningful commercial exposure

This is an environment for builders who want to see their systems used, trusted, and scaled.

What We’re Looking For

You have already built systems that operate reliably in production environments.

You are comfortable working with:
- incomplete information
- imperfect data
- unclear problem definitions
- fast iteration cycles
- high-stakes decision environments

You move quickly.
You think commercially.
You care about measurable outcomes.

You are likely stronger at shipping than presenting.

Technical Background

You will probably have strong experience across several of:
- Python and modern ML infrastructure
- LLM systems and agent architectures
- orchestration, retrieval, and evaluation systems
- reinforcement learning or optimisation systems
- event-driven or streaming systems
- low-latency production environments
- real-time inference infrastructure

Experience in trading, betting, or probabilistic decision systems is useful but not required.

We care more about judgement, speed, and the ability to build systems that survive contact with reality.

What Success Looks Like

Within the first few months, we would expect:
- multiple systems deployed into live workflows
- measurable improvements against baseline performance
- tighter decision loops
- increased execution consistency
- systems trusted by operators in production

Success in this role is measured by commercial impact.

Environment

- Small team
- High trust
- Fast execution
- Direct communication
- High standards
- Strong ownership culture

We hire carefully and prefer a small number of exceptional people over scaling headcount.

Compensation

Compensation is flexible and designed to attract exceptional individuals.

Job Title: Employee Experience and Design Thinking Advisor
Salary: EX work that drives real behaviour change
Ad Text:
Are you an EX specialist who brings structure, creativity and strong facilitation to complex challenges?   

Design and facilitate experiences that turn change into everyday ways of working from culture to AI adoption.
Work on impactful projects with ASX-listed clients, solving complex business challenges through people
Hybrid role: Balance home with our architect-designed central Sydney HQ.
As part of our small, multidisciplinary team at We Are Unity, you’ll bring innovative thinking and exceptional delivery to some of Australia’s most complex transformation projects - designing experiences, facilitating conversations and helping organisations turn strategy into action.

Life with us means partnering with progressive organisations, including ASX-listed clients, to realise their Purpose and drive change that unites strategy, brand, and culture. We create the human conditions for organisations to perform: practical, grounded work that solves real business problems and makes a genuine difference, without the weight of big bureaucracy.

The Opportunity
You’ll lead discrete workstreams under a Project Lead, owning your deliverables, facilitating key moments with clients, and keeping your stream moving with quality and pace.

What you’ll be doing:

Experience Design & Co-design: Understand moments that matter, identify friction points, and apply design thinking to co-create practical interventions that improve how work gets done, aligning employee experience, customer experience, brand, culture and performance with client strategy.
Facilitation & Engagement: Design and facilitate workshops, interviews and working sessions that surface insight, build alignment and move clients towards clear decisions and action.
AI & Transformation Design: Shape the human side of transformation, including AI adoption - translating complex strategy into practical, usable ways of working that teams can apply day-to-day.
Insight to Action: Support diagnostics through interviews and workshops, synthesising insights into clear problem statements, design principles and practical recommendations for senior stakeholders.
Delivery & Problem Solving: Own workstreams end-to-end, bringing structure to ambiguity, solving problems as they arise and delivering high-quality outputs at pace.
Client Growth Contribution: Identify opportunities to deepen impact (e.g. experience design, leader enablement, adoption support) and contribute to ongoing client work.
Who we’re looking for:
5+ years’ experience in employee experience, design-thinking, transformation, change or related fields (consulting, agency, or in-house).
Strong in design thinking - able to move from insight to practical, usable solutions.
Confident facilitator, able to guide groups, manage dynamics and drive outcomes.
Excellent structuring and communication skills - you can make complexity simple and actionable.
Comfortable working in ambiguity, juggling priorities and owning delivery.
Naturally curious and thoughtful, enjoys solving business problems through people, behaviour and experience.
Nice to have: Experience in culture transformation, behavioural change, operating model change, or supporting AI/tech adoption from a human-centred design lens.
Why join us?

Permanent, hybrid role (with potential for a 4-day week for the right candidate) based in Central Sydney.
Meaningful work with progressive clients alongside a talented, multidisciplinary team.
A steep learning curve in a niche space, stretching your craft across culture transformation, performance and AI adoption.
Work that uniquely blends strategy, design, facilitation and human behaviour.
Sound like you?
If you’re excited by designing and facilitating meaningful change, and enjoy turning complex challenges into practical, human-centred solutions, we’d love to hear from you.

Company Description

Bapcor Ltd is the leading automotive aftermarket provider of accessories, equipment and service. We host one of the widest ranges of car parts in the world, with brands including Burson, Autobarn, Midas, Truckline, Premier Auto Trade and Baxters.

We’re more than just automotive — we’re a dynamic, purpose-led organisation powered by people. Our Melbourne-based corporate team plays a critical role in shaping the strategy and success of the Bapcor Group.

In joining our team, you’ll be welcomed into a supportive business that spans more than 1,000 locations with over 5,000 team members across Australia, New Zealand and Thailand. We are passionate about our team, with safety and inclusivity at the forefront of everything we do. We’re also passionate about our customers, products, and living our values of:

Doing the right thing
Being in it together
Giving a damn
Getting it done
Job Description

Bapcor is seeking an experienced and commercially minded Reward Partner to join our People & Culture team.

Working closely with our Head of Reward & Benefits, this is a key role where you will partner with the business to design and deliver fit-for-purpose reward solutions, ensuring alignment with organisational strategy, market competitiveness, and regulatory requirements.

As a Reward Partner, you will play an important role in shaping and delivering Bapcor’s reward, benefits, and performance frameworks. Working closely with stakeholders across the business, you will provide expert advice and insights to support decision-making, drive consistency, and enhance employee value propositions.

 

Key Responsibilities

Consult with business leaders to design and implement total reward solutions
Drive the implementation and ongoing governance of Job Architecture and Job Evaluation frameworks
Lead and embed the performance and remuneration review cycle across the organisation
Support Executive Reward programs, including equity plans and annual report disclosures
Manage and promote Bapcor’s benefits portfolio, collaborating with internal stakeholders and external vendors
Collaborate on governance relating to modern award/Enterprise Agreement compliance and pay equity/compression considerations
Support the design and delivery of incentive frameworks across the business
Lead and support key initiatives including WGEA reporting and gender pay equity programs
Provide relevant analysis, data visualisation and insights to support commercial decision-making
Qualifications
Degree-qualified in HR, Business,or a related analytical discipline
GRP (Global Remuneration Professional) designation highly regarded
6+ years’ experience in Total Reward, Compensation & Benefits, Incentives including sales incentives  and/or Governance
Strong analytical capability with experience in data modelling, insights, and reporting
Advanced Excel skills (essential) and experience with Power BI or similar tools (desirable)
Experience developing and implementing policies and change programs
Strong stakeholder engagement and consulting capability, with the ability to influence
Additional Information

Why join Bapcor?

At Bapcor, we focus on what matters most for our customers, team members and the communities we serve. We go the extra mile to get the job done and always live our values.

In joining our team, you can access:

Diverse career opportunities within the Bapcor Group
Discounts across Bapcor products and services, including our Retail and Trade brands
Industry leading policies to support your work/ life balance (gender neutral parental leave, purchase leave, volunteer leave etc…)
Paid birthday leave
Wellbeing support
Novated leasing and more

We pride ourselves on our inclusive work environment, and encourage applications from people of all ages, cultures, gender identity, abilities and backgrounds, providing a recruitment experience that’s fair, free from bias and accessible. If you need to inquire about reasonable accommodation for the application process, please email car••••@bapcor.com

 

No Recruitment Agencies Please!

While we understand that recruitment agencies would love to offer their support, we kindly request all agencies to refrain from contacting us regarding this job posting. All applications should be submitted directly by candidates.

What if you could do the kind of work the world needs?

At WSP, you can access our global scale, contribute to landmark projects and connect with the brightest minds in your field to do the best work of your life. You can embrace your curiosity in a culture that celebrates new ideas and diverse perspectives. You can experience a world of opportunity and the chance to shape a career as unique as you.

This role will see you enhance your capabilities and the reputation of WSP’s Mining team. You will collaborate with talented professionals working on iconic projects to drive future ready outcomes that will benefit clients and communities.

At WSP you’ll find the scale and reach you need to do the kind of work the world needs – and the culture and people that make it the best work of your life.

The Role

We are seeking an experienced Study Manager to support our Mining & Metals business in delivering front-end studies and due diligence projects. This role will provide project management and technical coordination across multi-disciplinary teams, working closely with both internal business units and external stakeholders.

You will play a key role in leading and delivering high-quality study outcomes, from early-stage scoping through to Pre-Feasibility and Definitive Feasibility Studies.

Lead and coordinate mining studies and due diligence projects (Scoping, PFS, DFS)

Manage and deliver peer reviews and independent assessments

Coordinate multi-disciplinary proposals for studies and due diligence assignments

Provide leadership to project teams to ensure quality, consistency and timely delivery

Oversee cost estimate reviews and financial model inputs

Manage study scheduling, budgets, and reporting requirements

Drive integration across key technical disciplines
 

About You

Background in Process Engineering (preferred) with exposure to mining study environments

Proven experience delivering front-end studies within mining or heavy industry

Strong ability to lead multi-disciplinary teams and drive outcomes

Commercial awareness and ability to interpret project financials and cost models

Technical Capability

Working knowledge across major mining disciplines to enable effective technical integration

Familiarity with JORC, NI 43-101 and S-K 1300 reporting standards

Understanding of study phase requirements and toll-gate processes

Experience with AUSIMM and AACE cost estimation frameworks

Exposure to;

Commodity markets and off-take arrangements

Cost and commodity benchmarking

Basic financial modelling and project valuation


Why WSP?

Work on major mining projects across Australia and globally

Collaborate with high-performing, multidisciplinary teams

Opportunity to influence early-phase project decisions and strategy

Flexible working environment and strong focus on employee development

If you're looking to step into a role where you can lead studies, influence project direction, and work across the full mining value chain, we’d like to hear from you.

Randstad Sourceright is proud to be partnering with and working alongside a leading Australian financial services organisation dedicated to providing retirement security and excellence in superannuation management.





Are you an analytical and detail-oriented professional passionate about improving data reliability and transparency? We are looking for a Data Business Analyst to join the Transformation and Technology business unit. This is a key role focused on translating business needs into actionable data and system requirements, ensuring our data solutions are accurate, trusted, and aligned with organisational standards. It is an ideal opportunity for a proactive team member who enjoys mapping complex data flows, documenting specifications, and partnering with stakeholders to solve data management issues at the root cause.




Role: Data Business Analyst

Location: Canberra or Sydney (hybrid)

Job Type: contract - confirmed until Dec 26, ongoing after EOFY approval

Start Date: ASAP




Responsibilities:

Requirements & Documentation: Gather and document business requirements and operational processes to support data initiatives, translating business needs into clear specifications for solution design.

Data Analysis & Integrity: Analyse and validate datasets to identify management issues, uncover root causes, and provide recommendations to improve data accuracy and integrity.

Flow Mapping & Lineage: Map end-to-end data flows and lineage across systems to support impact analysis, solution design, and regulatory requirements.

Workshop Facilitation: Lead workshops with business and data owners to define Critical Data Elements (CDEs), data controls, quality thresholds, and data interpretations.

Stakeholder Collaboration: Partner closely with data engineers, architects, and product teams to ensure solutions align with strategic objectives and organisational standards.

Testing & Adoption: Support testing, deployment, and change management by preparing test cases, validating outputs, and ensuring the smooth adoption of new data solutions.

Governance Support: Develop and maintain data-related policies, standards, and procedural documentation.





Experience:

Industry Expertise: Business analysis experience, focused on data management, data governance, or a related discipline.

Industry Knowledge: Experience within the Australian Superannuation or wider Financial Services sector is highly regarded.

Technical Proficiency: Strong skills in writing SQL queries to extract and validate data, proficiency in BI tools (PowerBI/Tableau), and advanced Excel (lookups, pivots, modelling).

Core Competencies: Proven ability to map business workflows (current state → future state) and a solid understanding of data modelling fundamentals, CDE concepts, and metadata.

Communication & Influence: Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to simplify complex data concepts and facilitate productive stakeholder relationships.




Please apply below and we will be in touch!

The primary purpose of this role is to build and maintain the Quality assurance program to ensure internal stakeholders and external vendors are adhering to the funds core values and brand model. This is more than a governance role — you will actively shape how we deliver for our members. By identifying opportunities across process, systems, and capability, you’ll drive meaningful improvements that enhance decision quality, efficiency, and the overall member experience.

About you

You are a quality-driven insurance professional with deep technical claims expertise and a passion for continuous improvement, bringing a strong governance mindset and the ability to translate insights into better outcomes for members.

Key responsibilities

Provide technical claims expertise and guidance across insurance and claims-related matters

Experience in a similar quality, technical or governance-focused role

Extensive experience in claims and insurance administration, including Death and TPD claims
Demonstrated experience in quality assurance frameworks, audits and governance model design

Morgan McKinley are partnering exclusively with a Global Bank as they expand their SOX team in Sydney. We're on the hunt for a highly experienced Senior SOX and Risk Analyst to join the Finance team, working Hybrid.

This is a Day Rate Contract running until 29/01/27 but the goal is to convert to a permanent role.

A key part of this role will be operational risk management, looking at ways to improve control and strengthen the risk environment in relation to SOX. You will provide first line support to internal and external stakeholders in response to operational risk and ensure that the finance department is compliant with the Global Bank's standard.

This role would ideally suit an experienced auditor who has had a focus on risk and control around SOX, looking for their next challenge.

Responsibilities:

Develop, implement and maintain the operational risk and governance framework, ensuring internal controls align across the frameworks.
Take ownership of the Controls Scoping, Risk Controls Self Assessments (RCSAs) and Test of Design Walkthroughs. Be the point of contact for all SOX testing, ensuring that all controls are tested regularly and that necessary changes are made.
Provide support to the first line testers and second line monitors from both a process and documentation perspective.
Work closely with external auditors, process owners, senior management and the operational risk teams. Coordinate with offshore counterparts who also conduct a lot of testing for Australia.
Optimise controls from Manual to Automated, identify internal control deficiencies and create and implement corrective actions for processes in SOX scope.

Requirements:

Chartered Accountant with 5-10 years experience working within financial services and operational audits.
Proven experience in a hands-on SOX Compliance role.
Successful project management skills, excellent communication both written and verbal and the ability to work autonomously to deliver within tight timeframes.
Strong analytical and Excel skills, a great attention to detail and a passion for problem solving.
An autonomous worker with confidence in dealing with key stakeholders across different business units to build relationships and influence.

If this opportunity sounds aligned to your career goals, then reach out to Hannah Sykes today for a confidential discussion as the client is looking to interview as soon as possible!

Please only apply if you hold full Australian Working Rights. Only successful candidates will be contacted.

About Us

Biointelect is a leading life science consultancy that partners with global clients to deliver strategic insights and comprehensive solutions across the life science ecosystem.

Our mission, Bringing Innovations to Life, Step by Step, ensures our clients are supported every step of the way, with deep expertise ensuring their life science innovation is ready for successful commercialisation. Our team is dedicated to delivering evidence-based solutions that drive successful market access and patient outcomes.

About the role

As a Senior Manager within Biointelect’s Commercialisation, Access and Policy team, you will play a central role in helping life science innovators navigate the journey from early innovation through to commercial success.

Working across pharmaceutical, biotech and health technology clients, you will lead strategic commercialisation projects spanning opportunity assessment, market access, pricing, launch readiness and broader market strategy. This is a highly collaborative consulting role suited to someone who combines strong strategic capability with real-world industry experience and enjoys working closely with clients to solve complex challenges.

You’ll partner with senior stakeholders, lead multidisciplinary project teams, contribute to business growth through client engagement and opportunity development, and help shape the future of healthcare innovation across Australia and globally.

Key Responsibilities

Lead the development and delivery of commercialisation strategies across the product lifecycle, including market access, pricing and launch readiness
Partner with pharmaceutical, biotech and health technology clients to provide strategic, evidence-based commercial advice
Lead complex consulting projects, ensuring high-quality delivery, stakeholder alignment and project excellence
Build trusted relationships with senior stakeholders and facilitate strategic discussions and workshops
Contribute to business development activities including client briefings, proposal development and converting opportunities into long-term client engagements
Provide leadership, mentoring and development support to direct reports, contributing to a collaborative and high-performing team culture
Apply industry and market insights to support client outcomes, thought leadership and continuous improvement initiatives

To be successful in the role, you’ll have

Significant experience in management consulting, ideally within life sciences, healthcare or pharmaceutical strategy
Direct pharmaceutical or biotech industry experience across commercial, medical, market access, policy or strategic functions will be highly regarded
Demonstrated experience leading complex, multi-stakeholder projects and high-performing teams
Strong strategic thinking and analytical capability, with the ability to translate complexity into actionable insights
Excellent communication, facilitation and stakeholder engagement skills
A collaborative leadership style with a genuine passion for mentoring and developing others
A strong understanding of the Australian healthcare and life sciences environment

Why Join Biointelect?

You’ll join a purpose‑driven organisation that sits at the intersection of science, strategy and impact. You’ll work alongside and lead industry experts on cutting-edge projects that shape the future of health technology. We value innovation, strategic thinking, and a commitment to improving patient outcomes. This is a unique opportunity to utilise your consulting career in a team that blends scientific rigour with commercial strategy.

If you’re passionate about life sciences and want to make an impact, we welcome your application.

Please include your resume and a brief cover letter outlining your suitability for the role.

Biointelect is an equal opportunity employer. All applicants will be considered for employment without attention to race, colour, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, veteran, or disability status.

Ad Text:
Shape your future with a career at Shell Energy.

Shell Energy combines deep expertise and true innovation to help our electricity and gas customers navigate a rapidly changing market. We’re seeking people who bring the drive to succeed in providing the energy businesses and households need for today and tomorrow.

As the Pricing Manager, you will lead the design, implementation and optimisation of retail energy pricing across our B2C portfolio. Reporting to the Head of Commercial Strategy and based in Melbourne, this role plays a critical part in driving margin performance, supporting growth, and ensuring pricing remains competitive, compliant and customer‑aligned in a dynamic energy market.

You’ll combine deep analytical capability with strong commercial judgement, partnering closely with Product, Finance, Trading, Regulatory and Customer teams to deliver robust pricing strategies and insights that help shape business decisions.

This is an individual contributor role with significant influence across the organisation.




With respect, integrity and honesty you will:

Lead the development and ongoing optimisation of retail pricing strategies across the portfolio

Build, maintain and enhance pricing and financial models to support margin optimisation, cost recovery and strategic decision‑making

Conduct and lead periodic price reviews in response to changes in wholesale markets, network charges and regulation

Define pricing inputs and strategies for new product launches, acquisition campaigns and offer design in partnership with Product teams

Drive improvements in gross margin management through cohort‑specific cost stacks and enhanced cost transparency

Provide pricing guidance, commercial insights and analytical support to Sales, Marketing and Product teams

Analyse customer usage and consumption patterns, including carbon and green products and distributed energy resources

Ensure pricing complies with energy retail regulation, including standing and default market offer obligations where applicable




Who you’ll work with:

Pricing Operations to implement price changes accurately and efficiently

Trading and Retail Middle Office to reflect appropriate wholesale costs in customer pricing

Finance and Commercial Strategy to support forecasting and long‑term financial outcomes

Regulatory and Compliance teams to manage regulatory change and obligations

Customer teams to ensure pricing aligns with customer value propositions




Here’s what you’ll need to thrive in this role:

You’re a commercially minded pricing professional who thrives in complexity and ambiguity. You bring:

Extensive experience in pricing, commercial analytics or revenue management

Experience in energy pricing (particularly retail energy) with complex customer portfolios highly regarded

Advanced financial modelling capability, including scenario and sensitivity analysis

Proven experience translating complex cost structures (e.g. network tariffs) into customer pricing

Attention to detail paired with the ability to think strategically

Confident written, visual and verbal communication skills, with the ability to influence senior stakeholders

A collaborative, proactive mindset and the confidence to challenge the status quo

Bachelor’s degree in Commerce, Finance or a related technical discipline or equivalent




Why join our team

Every day, our people bring deep expertise, true innovation to help our customers meet their energy goals.




Here are the top reasons why out people choose us

The  Australian Health Service Alliance  acts on behalf of 20 small to medium private health funds across Australia. They manage provider relationships, negotiate contracts with hospitals and specialists, and facilitate the collection and analysis of hospital and medical data as such, they are looking for a Data Engineer / Senior Technical BA Hybrid to join their IT team in Kew.

You’ll sit at the centre of their bespoke internal systems, 20 member health funds, external vendors and development teams, working across complex health datasets and integrations in a highly regulated environment.
  
Key Responsibilities:

Work across the full data delivery lifecycle – from requirements through to implementation
Lead stakeholder workshops, translating business problems into data-driven solutions
Act as the technical bridge between data sources, systems, and stakeholders
Design and document data flows, mappings, and transformations across multiple systems
Own data integration and migration activities (XML, JSON, CSV, TXT)
Perform hands-on data analysis using SQL across complex relational datasets
Support and enhance Power BI reporting (data modelling, DAX, dashboards)
Collaborate with engineering teams on API integrations and data pipelines (Azure environment)
Drive UAT and validation from a data integrity perspective
Identify opportunities to improve data quality, structure, and reporting capability
  
Key Requirements:

7-10+ years in a hands-on data-focused role (Data Engineer, Technical BA, Analytics Engineer)
Strong experience working with structured and semi-structured data (XML, JSON, CSV, TXT)
Advanced SQL skills – complex querying, data transformation, schema understanding
Solid experience in data modelling (star schemas, relationships, optimisation for reporting)
Proven experience with Power BI (data models, DAX, dashboard development)
Experience working on data integrations, APIs, and cloud platforms (Azure preferred)
Ability to translate between business needs and technical/data solutions
Exposure to healthcare datasets (HCP, MBS, DRG, ICD) – highly desirable but not mandatory if data skills are strong
Background in development (.NET, Angular) or engineering is a strong advantage
The Type of Person Who Will Thrive In the role

Someone who has come from a development or data-heavy background and naturally leans into the technical side of analysis.
You’re self-led. You don’t wait to be told what to do. You bring ideas to the table.
You’re not interested in simply ticking requirement boxes, you want to understand the “why”, challenge thinking where needed, and contribute to building better systems.
Most importantly, you want to be part of a growth journey, helping shape and strengthen AHSA’s technology landscape as it evolves.
  
If that sounds like you, we’d love to hear from you.
Our passion for people, technology, and commitment to being experts in our industry specializations is what sets us apart in the marketplace. 

For more information, please contact Bianca Rivera at bri••••@synchropartners.com.au

THE OPPORTUNITY

At SkinKandy, we are the piercing professionals, and the industry leader in body piercing and jewellery. Uniquely growing at a rapid rate, opening multiple new stores every year across Australia and New Zealand, with international expansion being the next chapter; this is the moment to join our dynamic business!

THE ROLE

We’re hiring an FP&A Manager to own the financial planning that is taking us through the next phase of growth. Reporting to the Head of Finance, you’ll work shoulder-to-shoulder with the CFO and have regular line of sight to the CEO.

This is a genuinely autonomous seat. You’ll own the models, the forecasts, the scenario work and the commercial narrative that sits behind them. It’s an opportunity where the quality of your thinking shows up in the decisions the business makes, new store economics, expansion geographies, capex prioritisation, pricing, and mix.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

Own the financial models end-to-end: build, maintain and evolve the three-statement model, driver-based forecasts and the rolling re-forecast process.

Lead the annual budget and long-range planning cycles, including new store roll-out modelling, unit economics and payback analysis.

Build the reporting layer in Power BI on top of the data lake — turning raw transactional data into board-ready insight the exec team actually uses.

Partner with the CFO and Head of Finance on board materials, investor reporting and strategic analysis, including work that supports the group’s capital markets readiness.

Run scenario and sensitivity analysis on international expansion, new market entry, capex decisions and pricing/mix initiatives.

Be the trusted commercial sparring partner to operations, retail and marketing; translating the numbers into decisions and actions.

Drive continuous improvement across FP&A processes, reporting cadence and the underlying data model.

Set and report on labour and revenue targets and various other operational metrics

Continuous cost base analysis

Opportunities for business partnering projects

Ad-hoc and ever evolving reporting requirements depending on the needs of the business – no week will look the same as the last!

 ABOUT YOU

CA/CPA qualified (or equivalent) with a strong FP&A, commercial finance or investment banking background.

Exceptional financial modelling skills — you can build a driver-based model from a blank sheet and defend every line of it.

Comfortable working across a modern data stack — SQL and/or data lake exposure, and genuine fluency in Power BI (DAX confidence is a big plus).

Commercially curious. You ask why the numbers are what they are, not just what they are.

Experience in fast-growth, high-change environments. PE-backed, retail, multi-site or pre-IPO experience is highly regarded.

Self-directed and comfortable operating with autonomy at pace — this role suits someone who wants to build, not maintain.

Flexible in all aspects – if you are asked to dive into something new and without precedence, you will be someone who enjoys the experience and thrives in a problem solving environment.

LOCATION

This role is based onsite at SkinKandy’s HQ in Maroochydore on the Sunshine Coast. Sunshine, surf, and one of the best lifestyle-to-career trade-offs in the country; working in a business with genuine national and international ambition.

INTERESTED IN JOINING THE PIERCING PRO'S AND BE A PART OF ANZ'S FASTEST GROWING RETAILER?

We’d love to hear from you. Apply with your CV, or reach out for a confidential conversation to learn more about the role, the business and the journey ahead.

Job Title: Commercial Finance Transformation Lead (6 Month FTC)
Ad Text:
Western Sydney | Onsite Parking | Hybrid Working
6-Month Fixed Term Contract (Potential for Extension/Permanency)
Suitable for Commercially Minded Finance Leaders with Passion For Transformation
About the Company and Role
We are currently partnering a leading industrial manufacturing group  to recruit a Commercial Finance Transformation Lead on a 6-month fixed-term contract. This is a fantastic opportunity for a commercially minded finance leader with a passion for transformation, operating model design, and building best-in-class FP&A capability. This is a high-impact, hands-on role where you will play a critical part in shaping the Commercial Finance operating model, driving planning capability, and enabling better decision-making across the business.
  
Key Responsibilities

Design and implement the Commercial Finance operating model including FP&A and Business Partnering structures
Establish planning, forecasting, and performance management frameworks (budgeting, rolling forecasts, long-range planning)
Define and support implementation of systems, reporting, and planning tools
Partner with ERP and data teams to ensure fit-for-purpose financial systems and reporting capability
Drive stakeholder engagement and change management across divisional leadership teams
Support the rollout of new planning and reporting platforms
Build governance frameworks, reporting cadence, and KPI structures
Assist in recruitment and onboarding of the permanent Commercial Finance team
Develop and execute a transformation roadmap, tracking delivery against key milestones
  
About You
You will bring:

CA/CPA preferred with ~15+ years’ experience across commercial finance, FP&A, and/or transformation roles.
Hands-on finance transformation experience with ability to operate both strategically and tactically in complex environments
Strong commercial finance and business partnering capability
Experience implementing planning, forecasting, or ERP/reporting systems
Ability to navigate complex, multi-entity environments
Strong stakeholder engagement and influencing skills
Experience supporting team build-out and capability uplift
  
Why Apply?

Shape a finance function from the ground up
Work directly with an experienced and high-performing leadership team
Exposure to a large-scale, multi-entity transformation
A fast-paced, high-impact environment where your work will deliver tangible outcomes
Potential for contract extension based on delivery milestones

How to Apply:
To apply, please click on the appropriate link below. Alternatively, for a confidential discussion, please contact Samantha Tan at sta•@sharpandcarter.com.au

The Company
Group FP&A Manager required for a large, well established Australian organisation with a significant national footprint. The business operates across a complex multi business unit structure and is currently strengthening its central finance function to support Group level planning, performance management and strategic decision making.
 
The Role
Reporting into the Senior Group FP&A Manager, you will sit within a centralised finance team responsible for delivering high quality Group reporting, planning and analysis. You will work closely with senior stakeholders across multiple business units, providing insight and challenge to support both operational performance and longer term strategic initiatives.
 
Your responsibilities will include:

Ownership and coordination of the Group five year planning process across P&L, balance sheet and cash flow
Delivery of Group budgeting, forecasting and monthly performance reporting cycles
Preparation of executive level and board reporting packs with clear commentary and insights
Development of financial models, scenario analysis and long range planning outputs
Support for investment evaluation, capital allocation and wider commercial decision making
Involvement in acquisition, divestment and due diligence activity as required
Business partnering with senior stakeholders across multiple functions to challenge assumptions and drive performance improvement
Ongoing development and enhancement of planning tools, reporting frameworks and finance systems
About You
We are looking for a qualified finance professional with strong experience in Group FP&A or commercial finance within complex, multi entity organisations. You will bring:

A recognised accounting qualification such as CA or CPA
Strong experience in Group level planning, forecasting and reporting
Advanced financial modelling capability across P&L, balance sheet and cash flow
Exposure to investment analysis, M&A or capital projects
Ideally experience with enterprise planning systems (TM1 or similar)
Strong stakeholder management skills with the ability to influence senior non finance audiences
A commercial, analytical and detail driven mindset with strong problem solving capability
How to Apply
If you are interested in hearing more, please apply via the link or email your resume to Lewis Black at lbl•••@fourquarters.com.au

Click here to discover what it's like to be a part of QBE Group  

The opportunity

The Senior Manager, Investments Sustainability will lead the strategic direction and delivery of QBE’s investment-related sustainability initiatives, with a focus on implementing the Climate Transition Plan. Reporting to the Head of Business Operations & Investments Sustainability, the role will embed the Net-Zero Investment Framework 2.0 into the investment process, incorporating climate data and risk scenario analysis into investment due diligence, while establishing governance, reporting, and assurance-ready documentation. It also involves managing sustainability frameworks and metrics, integrating them into business planning, and enhancing engagement through effective communication tools. The role includes responding to stakeholder queries, producing internal reports, and collaborating with the Group Head of Sustainability on cross-functional initiatives to advance QBE’s sustainability agenda.

About QBE  

At QBE, we get to the heart of what matters for our customers. And we do it all with a human touch.  

We’re an international insurer with more than 13,000 people working across 26 countries – which means we’re big enough for your ambitions, yet small enough for you to make a real impact. It’s an exciting time. We’re building momentum towards our vision to become the most consistent and innovative risk partner.

What if you could have a positive impact – at work and in the world? As part of the QBE team, you’ll get to spend every day working with people who are passionate, talented and kind.

Your new role

Define and lead the development of investment sustainability objectives, metrics, and targets in collaboration with key internal stakeholders to ensure alignment with strategic priorities.

Design and implement investment sustainability policies and procedures, embedding detailed quantitative and qualitative assessment criteria into the investment due diligence process.

Drive engagement initiatives, supporting their execution to promote QBE’s sustainability objectives across investment practices and stakeholder groups.

Oversee and validate third party data and  climate scenario analysis outputs, including financed emissions, quantitative modelling, qualitative assessments, internal and external reporting, methodology documentation, and audit and assurance materials.

Contribute to the development of clear and impactful sustainability communications, including internal and external reporting on key initiatives and progress. Foster strong relationships with internal and external stakeholders to advance sustainability objectives and ensure consistent messaging and collaboration. Be the first point of contact for Finance and Sustainability

Provide expert guidance and training to the investment team on sustainability-related topics, enhancing capability and awareness across the function.

Represent QBE in sustainability forums, advancing QBE’s sustainability agenda and advocacy priorities, influencing group and divisional teams and contributing to thought leadership and best practice sharing.

Monitor emerging standards, taxonomies, and industry practices, recommending updates to policies, processes, and technologies that enhance the accuracy, timeliness, and auditability of sustainability-related investment analytics and reporting.

About you

To be successful you will extensive experience in institutional investing and portfolio analytics, I bring deep expertise in ESG and climate integration, translating complex analytics into decision-ready insights for senior leadership; I have hands-on proficiency with portfolio emissions tools such as MSCI TPF/Climate Risk and Aladdin Climate, alongside a strong understanding of climate transition scenarios, pathways, and modelling methodologies.

Demonstrated expertise in interpreting complex quantitative and qualitative data, with the ability to deliver clear, strategic insights to senior leadership through compelling investment commentary and board-level reporting.

Exceptional written and verbal communication skills, with a proven ability to articulate sophisticated investment and sustainability concepts to diverse internal and external stakeholders.

Strong planning and workflow management capabilities, ensuring timely and effective execution of sustainability initiatives across investment processes.

Proven ability to build trusted relationships, influence decision-making, and collaborate across teams to drive sustainability outcomes.

Deep experience in developing and applying climate-related assessments within investment frameworks, alongside producing high-impact reporting for senior management and governance forums.

Benefits  

We offer a range of benefits to help provide holistic support for your work life, whatever your circumstances. As a QBE employee you will have access to and benefit from:

‘Hybrid Working’ – a mix of working from home and in the office to enhance your work/life balance

Free holistic wellbeing coaching, nutritional, confidential counselling, financial and legal advice

18 weeks’ gender-equal flexible leave for all new parents, including paid super

To learn more about benefits of working with us, click here.

Awards & Recognition

We value our employee’s experience with us and are proud to have been recognised for the following awards:

2025 Winner of Excellence in Diversity, Equity & Inclusion Award at the Insurance Business Australia Awards for our Respect@QBE program

2023 Most Inclusive Workplace at the Australian HR Institute (AHRI) Awards

Ranked in the top ten in the AAGE Top Graduate Employers 2025 for medium sized program as voted by graduates

To learn more about our achievements, click here.

How to Apply

If you’re looking for a career that combines your expertise and your empathy, click Apply today. Successful applicants will need to complete work rights, police and credit checks through our online provider as a condition of employment.

Your career. At the heart of it. 

QBE recognises the value of diverse perspectives and experience. We are committed to an inclusive workplace where everyone feels valued and respected for who they are. If we can provide support with access requirements, alternative work arrangements or you would like to connect with one of our networks please contact us at rec••••••••@qbe.com.

Company Information

Global technology business that continues to thrive and grow at a rapid rate. This is a unique and world leading innovative business. They operate in over forty countries and continue to broaden scope.

The business is highly progressive and dynamic. The team you will work with our talented, driven and proud of the business they impact on every day. The feedback from staff that work for this business is that the culture is outstanding. They offer a very flexible hybrid work model and are driven by outcomes and performance.

Responsibilities

The role reports to the APAC President and works closely with all Business Unit leaders. You will get the opportunity to work across multiple regions in APAC. The role will combine business intelligence, commercial analysis, and market strategy support, acting as a key advisor to senior leadership across the region.

The key focus areas of the role are -

Marketing & Revenue Analytics - Analyse marketing performance, track effectiveness of marketing spend and recommend data-driven improvements

Sales Performance Analysis - Monitor and analyse sales performance across APAC by region, industry, product, and salesperson. Develop dashboards to track performance and opportunities

Sales Campaign Monitoring - Track effectiveness of sales campaigns and initiatives. Provide insights into campaign performance and recommend optimisation strategies

Market & Industry Intelligence - Conduct market sizing, competitive analysis, and industry landscape research for new business opportunities. Engage with industry stakeholders and external data sources to support strategic planning

Executive & Strategic Support - Deliver ad hoc analysis and strategic insights to APAC President and senior leadership team. Support decision-making through data storytelling and commercial recommendations

Product & Platform Analytics - Collaborate with the Product Manager to analyse product sales performance. Identify product-level opportunities and performance gaps

Salesforce & BI Enablement – Analysis of data to identify trends and identify opportunities

Background

4 + years experience in Business Intelligence, Commercial Analytics, Strategy, or Revenue Operations type roles

Strong Salesforce reporting and analytics experience

Advanced Excel skills. Experience with BI Tools (Power BI, Tableau etc)

Strong analytical skills and ability to translate data into actionable business insights

Experience working in an international (multiple country) role

Experience in B2B SaaS, technology, security, or industrial sectors would be advantageous but not essential

Management consulting or corporate strategy background is also advantageous but not essential

Ability to translate data into business decisions rather than reporting alone

Benefits

New role due to sustained growth

Partner with senior stakeholders and make an impact

Highly commercial, analytical and data driven role

Terrific culture, great leadership and a collaborative team

A unique and highly successful global business

Hybrid role - they are outcome driven rather than location of work. Work from home 2 + days per week

As this role looks after four regions you will have the opportunity to travel to your locations (about 20% of the time)

Excellent salary package 150k to 160k + super

Career progression as the company and role evolves

If you are interested in this role and have the suitable experience please apply.

Exciting opportunity to join an organisation undergoing significant expansion across its operating asset portfolio.

With continued investment in systems, infrastructure and operational growth, the business is seeking a commercially minded Finance Systems Analyst who can bridge the gap between finance, technology and operations.

This is a highly visible role suited to someone who enjoys solving complex business problems, building scalable financial processes and partnering with senior leadership to support strategic decision-making.

Working closely with a variety of stakeholders, you will play a critical role in improving reporting capability, integrating systems and supporting the financial modelling and setup of newly acquired or established business units.

The Opportunity

This is not a traditional accounting role. It is a true hybrid position requiring strong tech capability alongside commercial and finance acumen.

You will be responsible for:

Writing SQL scripts from scratch to connect and integrate multiple systems
Building and maintaining financial models and P&Ls for new and expanding business operations
Supporting system transformation and automation initiatives across the organisation
Developing reporting solutions and dashboards that drive operational and financial insights
Partnering with Executive and operational stakeholders to influence business decisions
Improving data integrity, reporting accuracy, and process efficiency across finance systems
Assisting with budgeting, forecasting, and performance reporting frameworks
Identifying opportunities to streamline workflows and automate manual processes
About You

To succeed in this role, you will combine technical systems capability with strong communication and stakeholder management skills.
You will ideally have:

CA/CPA qualification (or working towards)
Experience in a Systems Accountant, Finance Systems, Commercial Finance, or similar role
Strong SQL capability with the ability to write queries and scripts independently
Experience integrating data across multiple systems and platforms
Advanced Excel skills with exposure to Power BI, VBA, or Python highly regarded
Strong commercial acumen and financial modelling capability
Confidence engaging with Executive-level stakeholders and translating technical information into commercial insights
A proactive mindset with the ability to thrive in a growing, evolving environment
Why Apply?
Join a business experiencing strong growth and portfolio expansion
Work on meaningful transformation and automation projects
Exposure to senior leadership and strategic initiatives
Opportunity to influence systems, reporting, and business decision-making
Collaborative and high-performing team environment
Long-term career growth and development opportunities
If you are a technically strong finance professional looking for a role that combines systems, commercial finance, and stakeholder engagement, this is an outstanding opportunity to make a genuine impact.

About Datacom We help organisations accelerate growth through CX, digital engagement, MarTech and AI-enabled experiences. We’re growing a high-impact ANZ practice and seeking a senior leader to shape and scale our capabilities.

Role overview We’re looking for a Consulting Director – Digital Engagement to grow our CX and digital engagement business in Australia and NZ. Bring a strong background in CX/marketing, senior market relationships, and a track record of delivering MarTech/CDP-enabled transformations. You’ll shape strategy, design architectures and roadmaps, drive new business, and lead delivery with cross-functional teams.

What you’ll do

Lead CX and digital engagement strategy; build business cases for digital, MarTech and AI change.
Design practical CX/MarTech roadmaps across lifecycle, channels, data, AI and operating model.
Architect end-to-end digital engagement using Salesforce, Adobe, Braze, HubSpot, Sitecore; integrate with CRM, contact centers and data platforms.
Shape CX AI and agents (chatbots, voicebots, decisioning) with governance for privacy and bias.
Drive new business via C-suite networks; collaborate with account teams to build pipeline and close work.
Lead or oversee delivery of marketing automation, CDP and AI programs; provide architectural oversight.
Support bids with solution options, reference architectures and benefits cases; help meet revenue/margin targets.
Build and coach a small CX/digital engagement team; evolve ANZ offers.
Build market presence through thought leadership and vendor partnerships.
Ensure health, safety and wellbeing in all activities.

What you’ll bring Knowledge

Strong CX/marketing background in mid-to-large organisations; experience in ANZ markets.
Deep MarTech/CRM/CDP understanding; omnichannel activation across email, mobile, web, social, paid and offline.
Hands-on with Salesforce (Marketing Cloud, Journey Builder, Service Cloud, Einstein) and Adobe Experience Cloud (AEP, Target, Analytics, Sensei); Braze, HubSpot or Sitecore a plus.
CX AI knowledge (intent, sentiment, propensity, decisioning) and integration with CRM/CDP/marketing platforms.
Commercial mindset: impact on revenue, cost to serve, churn and lifetime value; experience building benefits cases and budgets.

Experience

10+ years in consulting or senior CX/digital/marketing roles; strong ANZ senior relationships.
Proven MarTech/CDP-enabled transformation delivery; experience with platform-enabled architectures and multi-phase programs.
Transformation track record across large CX/digital initiatives.

Skills

Business development: open doors, build trust with senior executives, hit pipeline targets.
Delivery and governance: manage complex programs, budgets, schedules and performance.
Communication: clear, concise briefings to senior business/technical audiences; influence and stakeholder management.
Analysis and planning: diagnose issues and turn outcomes into actionable roadmaps.

Qualifications

Essential: Bachelor’s degree in business, marketing, IT, science, engineering, economics or related field.
Desirable: MBA; certifications in Salesforce, Adobe, Braze, DS/AI, digital marketing or CX.

Why join Datacom

Impact: shape and grow our CX and digital engagement business in ANZ.
Partnerships: work with leading brands and key vendor ecosystems.

How to apply

Datacom is an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees.

Landing & Conversion Optimisation

Design and implement a comprehensive behavioural analytics framework across Exchange landing pages — covering scroll depth, content engagement, CTA performance, drop-off attribution, and device and geo segmentation

Define the event taxonomy in partnership with Engineering and Data, ensuring every meaningful pre-sign-up user action is instrumented, labelled, and queryable at scale

Build and own real-time funnel dashboards that give the full team visibility into the landing-to-sign-up conversion journey

Conduct continuous analysis using session-recording and heatmap tooling (FullStory, Hotjar, Microsoft Clarity, or equivalent) to surface engagement patterns and conversion opportunities

Translate behavioural insights into hypotheses and work with Design and Content to optimise page layout, messaging hierarchy, trust signals, and CTA placement

Run a structured, high-velocity A/B testing programme across landing pages — targeting a minimum of four experiments per month once infrastructure is established

Evaluate and improve how content on Exchange pages creates clear hooks and logical pathways that guide high-intent visitors toward account creation

Onboarding Funnel (Sign-up to KYC Initiation)

Own conversion performance and UX quality at every step of the post-sign-up journey:

Email submission — CTA clarity, form design, and value proposition at the moment of commitment

Email verification — delivery reliability, link click-through rate, re-send UX, and expiry handling

Phone submission — international dialling UX, number format validation, and regional trust signals

Phone verification (OTP) — delivery speed and reliability, SMS fallback, retry logic, and expiry management

KYC initiation — clear expectation-setting on documents required, estimated time, and next steps, so users arrive at the KYC stage informed and prepared rather than surprised

Define conversion targets at each step, track actuals on a weekly cadence, and drive improvements through UX iteration and controlled experimentation

Ensure step-level drop-off is fully attributed to specific friction points rather than absorbed into aggregate funnel metrics

Work with Legal and Compliance to accommodate regional onboarding variations without creating dead-end experiences for users in specific markets

Sign-up Rewards

Design and own the reward mechanics tied to the sign-up event — welcome bonuses, sign-up campaign incentives, and promotional offers that activate at account creation

Work with Marketing and Campaigns to define reward value, eligibility criteria, and communication timing at the sign-up stage, ensuring rewards are positioned as a conversion trigger rather than an afterthought

Instrument reward redemption tracking to measure the direct impact of sign-up incentives on registration conversion rate and downstream funnel progression

Build fraud-resistance into reward mechanics at the sign-up boundary — specifically multi-accounting, disposable email sign-ups, and synthetic identity abuse of promotional offers

Own the clean handoff to the Verification & Activation PM: sign-up reward is claimed at account creation; the KYC completion, first-deposit, and first-trade reward ladder is owned downstream

Login & Authentication

Own the sign-in experience across web, iOS, and Android — targeting time-to-login below 10 seconds for returning users

Lead the Exchange's path toward passwordless-first authentication: passkeys (FIDO2/WebAuthn) as the strategic direction, with Face ID and on-device biometrics as the primary implementation layer, and passcode or password as a fallback rather than the default

Drive adoption of passkey and biometric authentication as leading-indicator metrics for the modernisation of the authentication stack

Own session management, device identification, and session hijacking protection in partnership with Security and Risk

Design and maintain account recovery flows that balance user access needs with ATO prevention

Ensure compliance with regional authentication regulations — including Strong Customer Authentication (SCA) — without introducing friction that degrades the experience for legitimate users

Architect guardrails against modern authentication abuse vectors, including AI-generated identity attacks and automated credential abuse, as a PM-driven design constraint from the outset

AI-Native Ways of Working

Use agentic AI tools — Cursor, Claude Code, Gemini, LibreChat, Glean, or equivalent — with human-in-the-loop practices to reduce the time from insight to experiment to shipped improvement

Champion AI-native product development practices across your squad
